Potomac Falls Health & Rehab Center

CCN: 495179 · STERLING, VA 20164 · Loudoun County

OverviewRatingsStaffingInspectionsDeficienciesFinancialOwnership & ChainFacility FeaturesRelatedAll Data

Overview

Address
46531 HARRY BYRD HIGHWAY, STERLING, VA 20164
Phone
7038345800
Certified beds
150
Avg daily residents
147 (98% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit corporation
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1988-03-08
Setting
Urban
3 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 3/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 3/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 4/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.70 3.96 3.61 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.66 0.69 0.64 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 1.10 0.88 1.08 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 1.94 2.39 1.89 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.76 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.07 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.32
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.49
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.23
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
32.5%
annual
RN turnover
48.0%
annual
Administrators departed
0
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.40
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
1.02
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2021-12-02 14 14 0 68 1 68
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2018-10-11 12 12 0 48 1 48
Total weighted health score
63.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (37)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

35 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 G/H/I Actual harm
1 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0553 Allow resident to participate in the development and implementation of his or her person-centered plan of care. D 2021-12-02 2022-01-11
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2021-12-02 2022-01-11
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. D 2021-12-02 2022-01-11
0582 Give residents notice of Medicaid/Medicare coverage and potential liability for services not covered. D 2021-12-02 2022-01-11
0623 Provide timely notification to the resident, and if applicable to the resident representative and ombudsman, before transfer or discharge, including appeal rights. D 2021-12-02 2022-01-11
0625 Notify the resident or the resident’s representative in writing how long the nursing home will hold the resident’s bed in cases of transfer to a hospital or therapeutic leave. D 2021-12-02 2022-01-11
0640 Encode each resident’s assessment data and transmit these data to the State within 7 days of assessment. D 2021-12-02 2022-01-11
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. E 2021-12-02 2022-01-11
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. D 2021-12-02 2022-01-11
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. E 2021-12-02 2022-01-11
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2021-12-02 2022-01-11
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. D 2021-12-02 2022-01-11
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2021-12-02 2022-01-11
0919 Make sure that a working call system is available in each resident's bathroom and bathing area. D 2021-12-02 2022-01-11
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2018-10-11 2018-11-23
0559 Honor the resident's right to share a room with spouse or roommate of choice and receive written notice before a change is made. D 2018-10-11 2018-11-23
0582 Give residents notice of Medicaid/Medicare coverage and potential liability for services not covered. D 2018-10-11 2018-11-23
0623 Provide timely notification to the resident, and if applicable to the resident representative and ombudsman, before transfer or discharge, including appeal rights. D 2018-10-11 2018-11-23
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2018-10-11 2018-11-23
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2018-10-11 2018-11-23
0660 Plan the resident's discharge to meet the resident's goals and needs. D 2018-10-11 2018-11-23
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2018-10-11 2018-11-23
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2018-10-11 2018-11-23
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. D 2018-10-11 2018-11-23
0726 Ensure that nurses and nurse aides have the appropriate competencies to care for every resident in a way that maximizes each resident's well being. D 2018-10-11 2018-11-23
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2018-10-11 2018-11-23
0157 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2017-03-23 2017-05-12
0246 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2017-03-23 2017-05-11
0250 Provide medically-related social services to help each resident achieve the highest possible quality of life. E 2017-03-23 2017-05-12
0252 Provide a safe, clean, comfortable and home-like environment; and allow residents to use personal belongings to the extent possible. E 2017-03-23 2017-05-11
0309 Provide necessary care and services to maintain or improve the highest well being of each resident . G 2017-03-23 2017-05-11
0323 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provide adequate supervision to prevent avoidable accidents. K 2017-03-23 2017-05-11
0328 Properly care for residents needing special services, including: injections, colostomy, ureostomy, ileostomy, tracheostomy care, tracheal suctioning, respiratory care, foot care, and prostheses. D 2017-03-23 2017-05-11
0371 Store, cook, and serve food in a safe and clean way. F 2017-03-23 2017-05-11
0441 Have a program that investigates, controls and keeps infection from spreading. E 2017-03-23 2017-05-11
0498 Make sure that nurse aides show they have the skills and techniques to be able to care for residents' needs. E 2017-03-23 2017-05-11
0518 Train all employees on what to do in an emergency, and carry out unannounced staff drills. D 2017-03-23 2017-05-11

Methodology & sources
  • Nursing Home Provider Info (nh-provider-info), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 14,703 rows.
  • Nursing Home Health Deficiencies (nh-deficiencies), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 418,972 rows.

  • Skilled Nursing Facility Cost Report (snf-cost-report), vintage 2023, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 14,120 rows.
